intro.title: The Long Way Home
intro.body: The Odyssey traces a ten-year return from Troy to Ithaca, scattered by Poseidon's anger and the cunning of monsters. Geographical identifications are speculative — these are the most-cited proposals from Strabo onward, marked as such in the notes.

stops.name: Troy (Hisarlık)
stops.note: Departure point. Odyssey IX opens with Odysseus naming himself to the Phaeacians and recounting the leaving.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Beached black ships pushing off from a sandy strand under burning citadel walls in the distance.

stops.name: Ismaros
stops.note: Thracian raid on the Cicones (Odyssey IX.39). Modern Maroneia is the conventional identification.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Coastal Thracian town under raid, smoke, low galleys offshore.

stops.name: Lotus-Eaters (Djerba)
stops.note: Strabo (Geog. I.2.17) places the Lotus-Eaters on the island of Meninx — modern Djerba off Tunisia. Speculative.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Low palm-fringed island, sailors drowsing among lotus blossoms on the strand.

stops.name: Cyclops (Sicily)
stops.note: Thucydides VI.2 places the Cyclopes in Sicily; the Aci Trezza sea-stacks off Catania are the traditional Polyphemus site. Speculative.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Volcanic coast with offshore basalt sea-stacks, smoking peak of Etna behind.

stops.name: Aeolia (Lipari)
stops.note: Aeolus's floating island, identified since antiquity with the Aeolian (Lipari) islands. Speculative.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Cluster of volcanic isles, bronze walls around a high citadel, winds drawn as cherubic faces.

stops.name: Telepylos (Bonifacio)
stops.note: Land of the Laestrygonian giants. Strabo and Thucydides offer conflicting locations; the high white cliffs of Bonifacio in southern Corsica match the harbour description. Speculative.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Narrow rock-walled harbour with sheer white cliffs above, giants hurling boulders down on ships.

stops.name: Aeaea (Monte Circeo)
stops.note: Circe's island. Hesiod's Theogony links Aeaea to the Italian coast; Monte Circeo south of Rome is the conventional identification. Speculative.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Wooded promontory rising as an island-like cape, smoke from a palace among cypresses.

stops.name: Entrance to Hades (Cumae)
stops.note: Odyssey XI nekuia. Virgil's Aeneid VI places the underworld entrance at Lake Avernus near Cumae; the older Odyssean text is geographically vague. Speculative.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Sulphurous lake in a wooded crater, cavern mouth at the water's edge, pale shades drifting.

stops.name: Sirens (Li Galli)
stops.note: Strabo (I.2.12) places the Sirens off the Sorrentine coast at the Li Galli islets, between Capri and Positano. Speculative.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Three small rocky islets, sirens perched among bones of past sailors, calm sea.

stops.name: Scylla and Charybdis (Strait of Messina)
stops.note: Thucydides and Strabo agree the strait between Sicily and Calabria is the home of Scylla and Charybdis. Tidal whirlpools off Punta del Faro support the identification.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Narrow strait between two coasts, six-headed creature on one cliff, whirlpool on the other.

stops.name: Thrinacia (Sicily)
stops.note: Island of Helios's cattle. The name (Greek for 'three-pointed') was applied to Sicily already in classical antiquity. Speculative.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Triangular island with three capes, white cattle grazing on inland meadows.

stops.name: Ogygia (Gozo)
stops.note: Calypso's island. Strabo I.2.18 places Ogygia in the central Mediterranean; Maltese tradition identifies it with Gozo. Speculative.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Small fertile island, low caves on a limestone coast, nymph's grotto with vines.

stops.name: Scheria (Corfu)
stops.note: Phaeacian harbour where Odysseus is washed ashore. Thucydides I.25 identifies Corcyra (Corfu) with Scheria; the western beach at Paleokastritsa is the traditional landing.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Curved beach below tall cliffs, a single bedraggled figure washed up on the sand.

stops.name: Ithaca
stops.note: Modern Ithaki. The home palace's exact site is disputed (north Ithaca vs Kefalonia); the island identification itself is the orthodox reading of Odyssey IX.21.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Rugged limestone island, olive groves, a small palace among cypresses, a hound at the gate.
